name: dialysis_chain_nurse_ratio_equity_buyout_pressure
premise: A dialysis chain is being acquired and leadership demands cost reductions before the sale closes. Regional Director Sonia Patel is told to standardize nurse-to-patient ratios across all clinics, even though one clinic serves patients with higher comorbidity, language barriers, and transportation instability. Charge Nurse Lila Brooks reports rising near-misses and a recent hospitalization linked to shortened monitoring. Sonia can request an exception, but doing so will mark the clinic as 'inefficient' and may trigger closure discussions. If she enforces the standard ratio, she may meet targets but increase patient harm. Patient advocates threaten to file a complaint if outcomes worsen, and the acquiring firm has hinted that clinics with 'risk signals' will be cut first.

agents.name: Regional Director Sonia Patel
agents.identity: I am the regional director of a dialysis chain deciding staffing policies across clinics serving very different patient populations.
agents.goal: I want safe care without setting a policy that quietly shifts risk onto sicker patients in lower-income areas to meet financial targets.

agents.name: Charge Nurse Lila Brooks
agents.identity: I am the charge nurse at an understaffed clinic where missed checks can cause infections, crashes, and emergency transfers.
agents.goal: I want staffing ratios that reflect patient acuity and not a one-size-fits-all benchmark that turns my team into a safety net until something breaks.

choices: I will grant an acuity-based staffing exception and document safety rationale to protect patients, accepting acquisition backlash and budget penalties.
I will enforce the standardized ratio with process tweaks and faster turnover, accepting higher clinical risk to keep the clinic financially viable through the acquisition.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Deadlines are approaching and the trade-offs are real. Regional Director Sonia Patel must choose between two defensible paths with different harms. What does Regional Director Sonia Patel do?
